title: Identify points
description: Identify points in the first quadrant of a coordinate plane.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the following coordinate plane to write the ordered pair for each point.** 



[[☃ image 1]]

Point | Ordered pair
- | - 
$\greenD{A}$ | $($ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] , [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $)$
$\purpleC{B}$| $($ [[☃ numeric-input 3]] , [[☃ numeric-input 4]] $)$
$\blueD{C}$| $($ [[☃ numeric-input 5]] , [[☃ numeric-input 6]] $)$
hints:
The **ordered pair** of a point tells us how to get to it from the origin.

[[☃ image 2]]

$\begin{array}{cc}
(\goldE{\text{distance right}},& \maroonD{\text{distance up}})\\
\swarrow&\searrow\\
\goldE{x\text{-coordinate}}&\maroonD{y\text{-coordinate}}
\end{array}$

We are asked to find the $\goldE{x\text{-coordinate}}$ and $\maroonD{y\text{-coordinate}}$.

To get to point $\greenD{A}$ from the origin, we move right to $\goldE{5}$ and up to $\maroonD{6}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

So, the ordered pair for point $\greenD{A}$ is $(\goldE5,\maroonD6)$.

To get to point $\purpleD{B}$ from the origin , we move right $\goldE{0}$ and up to $\maroonD{2}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

So, the ordered pair for point $\purpleD{B}$ is $(\goldE0,\maroonD2)$.

To get to point $\blueD{C}$ from the origin, we move right to $\goldE{4}$ and up to $\maroonD{1}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

So, the ordered pair for point $\blueD{C}$ is $(\goldE4,\maroonD1)$.

Point | Ordered pair
- | - 
$\greenD{A}$ | $(\goldE5,\maroonD6)$
$\purpleD{B}$| $(\goldE0,\maroonD2)$
$\blueD{C}$| $(\goldE4,\maroonD1)$

options.alt: A coordinate grid is labeled 0 through 6 on the x-axis and the y-axis. Three plot points are on different parts of the grid. Point A is 5 units to the right and 6 units up from the origin. Point B is 2 units up from the origin on the y-axis. Point C is 4 units to the right and 1 unit up from the origin.

So, the ordered pair for point $\purpleD{B}$ is $(\goldE3,\maroonD3)$.

Point B is 3 units to the right and 3 units up from the origin. Point C is 5 units to the right and 5 units up from the origin.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which ordered pair is *not* graphed below?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The **ordered pair** of a point tells us how to get to it from the origin.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{array}{cc}
(\goldE{\text{distance right}},& \maroonD{\text{distance up}})\\
\swarrow&\searrow\\
\goldE{x\text{-coordinate}}&\maroonD{y\text{-coordinate}}
\end{array}$
Let's label the graphed points.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\redD{(4,2)}$ was not graphed.  



[[☃ image 1]]

\goldE{x\text{-coordinate}}&\maroonD{y\text{-coordinate}}
\end{array}$
Let's label the graphed points.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\redD{(2,0)}$ was not graphed.  



[[☃ image 1]]

From left to right the first plot point is 1 unit from the origin on the x-axis. The second plot point is 1 unit to the right and 2 units up from the origin.
